channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: David Beckham and Family Cheer Spain to World Cup Semi-Final Victory Amid Meme Reaction
item.description: David Beckham, 51, and his wife Victoria, 52, supported Spain in their World Cup semi-final clash against France, with their family in attendance. The former Real Madrid and Paris Saint-Germain player...

item.title: Concerns Over Potential Return of Street Disorder and Drug Use on Granville Strip After World Cup
item.description: Following the conclusion of the FIFA World Cup in Vancouver, concerns have emerged about the potential resurgence of street disorder and open drug use on Granville Street. The Granville Pedestrian Zon...

item.title: IBM Shares Drop Sharply Amid Market Volatility
item.description: IBM shares experienced a significant decline, marking the largest single-day drop in the company's history. The stock fell by over 15% in after-hours trading, driven by disappointing quarterly earning...

item.title: Oyo State Governor seeks international review of abduction incident amid Senate disagreement
item.description: The Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde, and the state assembly have supported his call for an independent international investigation into the mass abduction incident in Oriire, despite the Senate's rej...

item.title: Ado's Public Meeting Sparks Political Debate and Speculation
item.description: A public meeting organized by Ado has ignited widespread debate and discussion across various political circles, raising questions about its timing and potential implications. While details of the gat...

item.title: US Conducts New Strikes on Iran Amid Hormuz Blockade
item.description: The United States has carried out additional military strikes against Iran as part of its ongoing efforts to enforce a blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, a strategically vital waterway in the Persian G...

item.title: Computer Scientist Argues Human Tacit Knowledge Makes True Human-Level AI Unattainable
item.description: A new book by computer scientist Peter J. Denning challenges one of the foundational assumptions that has guided artificial intelligence research since Alan Turing's influential 1950 paper. Denning ar...

item.title: Senator Rodante Marcoleta Transferred to Quezon City Jail in Connection with Plunder Case
item.description: Senator Rodante Marcoleta was officially transferred to the male dormitory of Quezon City Jail in Payatas on July 15, 2026, following a commitment order from the Sandiganbayan Third Division related t...

item.title: Northern Territory urges federal support for major gas pipeline to east coast
item.description: The Northern Territory has publicly criticized the Albanese government's proposed gas reservation system, arguing it lacks practicality for the region. Instead, territory officials are advocating for ...

item.title: World Bank Updates Africa's Income Classification for 2026
item.description: The World Bank has released its 2026 income classification update, categorizing African countries based on their gross national income per capita. Togo is the only nation on the continent to change cl...

item.title: Commission probes Idac's legal mandate in Khumalo corruption case
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry is examining whether the Investigating Directorate Against Corruption (Idac) acted within its legal mandate during the investigation into the Khumalo corruption case...

item.title: Doctor Killed in Robbery Near Karachi's Teen Talwar; Three Suspects Arrested
item.description: A young doctor, Dr. Akash Kumar, was shot dead during a robbery near Karachi's Teen Talwar area on Monday. The incident occurred when robbers intercepted his vehicle after he withdrew Rs5m from a priv...

item.title: Brigid Bazlen: The Tragic Life of a 1960s Hollywood Star
item.description: Brigid Bazlen (1944–1989) was an American actress and model whose striking beauty and youthful charm defined her brief career in the late 1950s and early 1960s. Born in Chicago, she began her acting j...

item.title: World Cup Commentary Faces Criticism Over Sensationalist Headlines
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding sensationalist headlines in sports journalism, particularly during major events like the World Cup. It highlights how clickbait titles, such as 'Sensa...

item.title: Afghanistan Women's Soccer Team Faces Political and Social Challenges
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing struggles faced by the Afghanistan Women's Soccer Team, particularly focusing on the exile of player Farkhunda Muhtaj. Despite the Taliban's ban on women's sports, th...

item.title: Anya Taylor-Joy stars in 'Lucky', a gripping seven-part thriller about a con artist's double life
item.description: The article reviews the Apple TV+ series 'Lucky', starring Anya Taylor-Joy as a cunning con artist caught in a web of betrayal and danger. After stealing millions with her husband, Lucky discovers he ...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte Advances with Legal Arguments and Witness Testimonies
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte continues to unfold with significant developments. On July 15, 2026, the Senate impeachment court addressed multiple motions, including the subpoen...

item.title: Shiv Sena Corporator Gets Bail After Hospital Assault Allegations
item.description: Ramesh Mhatre, a Shiv Sena corporator from Maharashtra's Dombivli, was granted bail after being arrested for allegedly assaulting doctors and hospital staff. The incident occurred on July 6 when Mhatr...

item.title: The 13th Dalai Lama's 1910 Escape to India and His Return to Tibet
item.description: The article chronicles the historical journey of the 13th Dalai Lama, Tenzin Gyatso, who fled Tibet for India in 1910, years before his successor, the 14th Dalai Lama, made a similar escape in 1959. I...

item.title: Rosemary Odinga's Snail Farming Venture Generates Millions in Kenya
item.description: Rosemary Odinga, daughter of late former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, has built a successful snail farming business in Kenya, earning millions annually. The article details her journey into helicultur...
